agenda_briefs.title: Dennis Back appointed Chief Election Officer for 2026 Burnaby election
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election, replacing Samantha Pellizzari who is on leave. The election is scheduled for Saturday, October 17, 2026, for Mayor, Councillor, and School Trustee positions. Funds are included in the 2026 Local General Election budget from the Operating Surplus & Stabilization Reserve.

key_dates.label: 2026 Burnaby General Local Election date

agenda_briefs.title: North Road BIA renewal blocked — petition threshold met
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a staff report certifying that petitions against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw are sufficient. 66 property owners representing 65 of 120 parcels (over 50% of parcels and assessed value) filed opposition. Staff recommend Council not proceed with adoption of the renewal bylaw, which would have run from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — if Council follows the staff recommendation, the BIA levy will not continue.

key_dates.label: Council vote on North Road BIA renewal — Burnaby

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority 2025 audited financials, 2026 budget, $2M operating funding
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider receiving the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) 2025 audited financial statements and 2026 operating budget. The BHA received a clean audit opinion from KPMG. As of Dec 31, 2025, the BHA reported an accumulated surplus of $7.52M, cash holdings of $31.75M (including a $25M GIC maturing Nov 2026), and $5.55M in tangible capital assets. Receipt of these documents satisfies conditions for the City to provide $2.00M in 2026 operating funding under Partnering Agreement No. 1, supporting BHA's initial housing projects at 10th Avenue/Newcombe Street and Marine Drive (Edmonds).

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby e-bike share pilot: parking zones, speed rules, operator fees
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider amendments to three bylaws to launch an e-bike share pilot and address the provincial e-scooter pilot. Key changes: shared e-bikes and e-scooters must park only in designated Shared Micromobility Parking Zones; operators need a valid City agreement; e-scooters are banned on major roads and arterials except in protected cycle lanes or multi-use pathways; no riding on sidewalks unless signed; no headphones in both ears; a bell is required; and riding in parks is allowed only in designated areas.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council decision on April 14, 2026 — if approved, prepare for new parking, riding, and operator rules that may affect your property or fleet.

key_dates.label: Burnaby council decision on e-bike/e-scooter bylaw amendments

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby must submit housing target progress report by April 14, 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing submission of Burnaby's Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act. The report covers the first six-month reporting period (Sept 1, 2025 – Feb 28, 2026) and shows 910 net new housing units completed against a Year 1 target of 1,536. The city must receive the report by council resolution within 45 days of the reporting period end (by April 14, 2026) and submit it to the province as soon as practicable after that.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time; this is a council decision on a mandated provincial report.

key_dates.label: Burnaby housing target progress report submission deadline

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby to join Building Benchmark BC Year 7 energy program
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing the City of Burnaby to participate in Year 7 of the Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) program, a voluntary provincial program that tracks and reports energy use in large buildings. If approved, the city would share building energy data with the province to help identify ways to reduce energy costs and emissions. The decision is not final — council will vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed now. Watch for council's decision — if approved, building owners may need to prepare energy data for future reporting.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: FortisBC natural gas right-of-way on Burnaby Mountain
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider granting a Statutory Right-of-Way (a legal permission to use a specific piece of land) to FortisBC Energy Inc. over parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. This would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas pipes and equipment on that land. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M modular four-plex pilot at 8315 14th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular four-plex housing pilot at 8315 14th Avenue. The three-storey, 5,319 sq ft building will include four affordable units (one accessible) with private outdoor space. The project is supported by $150,000 from Fortis BC and $200,000 from the National Research Council. Construction is expected to take 6–8 months, with a 50–60 year useful life. The City will own the land and building, leasing units through its rental portfolio.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this pilot could lead to more modular housing projects in Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby to hire external reviewer of city financial practices
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on a proposal to engage a qualified external reviewer to assess the City's financial planning, operating practices, and capital investment priority setting. The review aims to safeguard city assets and mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents, driven by a sharp decline in density bonus revenues and higher procurement costs.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 20-storey, 215-unit rental rezoning at 5035 Beresford Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to forward a rezoning application (REZ #25-11) for a proposed 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street. The project would include 215 market rental units (32 studio, 98 one-bedroom, 65 two-bedroom, 20 three-bedroom), 38 residential parking spaces (100% EV ready), 441 secured bicycle spaces, and 574.74 m² outdoor rooftop amenity area. The site is currently zoned M4 Special Industrial and would be rezoned to CD Comprehensive Development District. Prerequisites include a Housing Agreement, 1.9 m road dedication along Beresford Street, and various studies and covenants.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — if the rezoning moves forward, you may have a chance to comment.

agenda_briefs.title: Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth — final recommendations due June 23, 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council received an interim update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. The task force has identified strategic priorities and is validating them through a business community survey and 10 stakeholder interviews conducted March 13–April 2, 2026. The task force will reconvene April 16, 2026 for three additional sessions through end of May to develop actionable recommendations, which will be presented to Council on June 23, 2026. No decisions or new rules are being voted on yet.

key_dates.label: Task Force reconvenes for final sessions
Final recommendations presented to Council

agenda_briefs.title: Developer forfeits $1,363,500 security for public pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider what to do with a $1,363,500 security deposit that developer Oxford Properties has forfeited instead of building a promised public amenity pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court. The developer chose to give up the money rather than construct the pier, which was part of the original rezoning approval for the Riverbend Business Park. The City now holds the forfeited funds and is waiting for Council to decide how to use them for an alternative public amenity along the Fraser Foreshore or elsewhere in Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw up for final adoption — 5-year levy
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of Bylaw #14797, which would create a new Business Improvement Area (BIA) along Burnaby North Road. The bylaw would impose a local service tax on properties classified as Class 5 (light industry) or Class 6 (business/other) within the zone, based on assessed value. The total grant to the BIA association over the 5-year term (April 1, 2026 – March 31, 2031) is $1,755,147.81, with annual amounts ranging from $317,954.94 (Year 1) to $385,740.60 (Year 5). Enough property owners have submitted formal petitions against the proposal, so the outcome is uncertain.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if the bylaw passes, your property taxes could increase if you are in the proposed BIA zone.

Council received the Certificate of Sufficiency, which confirms that the required number of property owner petitions against the renewal were not met, allowing the BIA to continue. minutes_briefs.title: Council approved 2026 corporate maintenance matters for Burnaby Housing Authority
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the 2026 annual corporate maintenance matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA), which manages city-owned housing. Council authorized the annual general meeting to be handled through a unanimous shareholders resolution, set December 12, 2026 as the annual reference date, appointed KPMG as auditor until the next annual meeting, received the BHA's financial statements for the year ending December 31, 2025, and appointed directors as listed in the report. The Shareholder Representative was directed to sign any necessary resolutions to carry out these decisions.

minutes_briefs.title: Council received Burnaby Housing Authority's 2025 financial statements and 2026 budget
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has received the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) 2025 Annual Financial Statements and 2026 Annual Operating Budget. The financial statements are audited, meaning an independent accountant has checked them for accuracy. Council voted unanimously to accept the report for information only. This means no new rules or spending were approved — council simply noted the documents as a record of the BHA's financial position and planned spending for the coming year.

THAT the BHA’s 2026 Annual Operating Budget be received.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved bylaw amendments for e-bike and e-scooter pilots
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council unanimously authorized amendments to the Street and Traffic Bylaw, Parks Regulation Bylaw, and Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to launch the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program. The City Solicitor will now draft the specific rule changes to allow these shared electric devices to operate on city streets and in parks. This decision is final and the new rules will take effect once the amended bylaws are formally adopted.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check if your business operates near bike-share stations or scooter parking zones; prepare for new traffic and parking rules once the amended bylaws take effect.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.5 PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS TO ADDRESS E-BIKE SHARE PILOT PROGRAM AND PROVINCIAL ELECTRIC KICK SCOOTER PILOT PROGRAM
The General Manager Engineering submitted a report proposing amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the City.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 169
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward amendments to the following bylaws to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program:
• Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961;
• Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979; and
• Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009,

as described in the report titled “Proposed Bylaw Amendments to Address E-Bike Share Pilot Program and Provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program”, dated April 14, 2026.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council authorized submission of Burnaby's housing target progress report to BC
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to receive a 6-month interim progress report on the Provincial Housing Target Order and authorized the General Manager of Planning and Development to submit the required Housing Target Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. This is a routine reporting step required under the Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation — the provincial laws that set housing targets for municipalities. The decision was made at the April 14, 2026 council meeting and does not create new rules or costs for businesses.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.6 PROVINCIAL HOUSING TARGET ORDER 6-MONTH INTERIM PROGRESS REPORT
The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report presenting the Housing Target Order and obtain Council authorization to forward Burnaby’s Housing Target Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 170
THAT the report titled “Provincial Housing Target Order 6-Month Interim Progress Report”, dated April 14, 2026, be received for information; and
THAT the General Manager Planning and Development be authorized to submit the Housing Target Progress Report Form, as found in Attachment 2, to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved external review of Burnaby's financial practices
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved hiring an outside expert to review the city's financial planning, spending, and how it sets priorities for big projects. The goal is to protect the city's money, avoid big tax hikes, and keep costs down for businesses and residents. The reviewer will work with the Financial Management Committee and report directly to Council.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.1 FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL PRACTICES
The Financial Management Committee (FMC) submitted a report seeking Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 181
THAT engaging a qualified external reviewer to assess the City’s financial planning, operating practices, and capital investment priority setting, as discussed at the Financial Management Committee meeting held on March 17, 2026, be approved; and
THAT the reviewer liaise with the Financial Management Committee and deliver findings and recommendations directly to Council.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for 20-storey apartment at 5035 Beresford Street
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to advance a rezoning bylaw for a 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street. The project will include 215 residential units above underground parking. Council also authorized a Housing Agreement (a legal contract that sets rules for the development, such as affordability requirements) and set conditions that must be met before the bylaw gets final approval.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.2 PDC - REZ #25-11 - 5035 BERESFORD STREET - PROPOSED 20-STOREY APARTMENT DEVELOPMENT (ROYAL OAK COMMUNITY PLAN)
The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking Council authorization to forward REZ #25-11 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting. The purpose of the proposed zoning bylaw amendment is to permit the construction of a 20-storey multiple-family apartment development comprised of 215 residential units above underground parking.  
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 182
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-11 be prepared and advanced to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting;
THAT a Housing Agreement, if required, be authorized according to the terms outlined in Section 3.4 of the report titled “REZ #25-11 - 5035 Beresford Street – Proposed 20-Storey Apartment Development (Royal Oak Community Plan)”, dated March 31, 2026, of the Planning and Development Committee meeting, and the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward a Housing Agreement Bylaw; and
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report be established as prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-11.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council abandons North Road BIA renewal after petitions
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to abandon the Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026. This decision was made because enough property owners signed petitions against the bylaw, meeting the legal threshold required to stop it. A Business Improvement Area (BIA) is a zone where local businesses pay an extra levy to fund improvements and marketing. Because the required number of property owners objected, the bylaw cannot move forward and the BIA renewal is cancelled.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1 #14797 - Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026
Reminder regarding the petition related - 7.2 the petitioner has met the legislative threshold - bylaw will be abandoned  
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 185
THAT due to sufficient petitions received by the City against Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026, Bylaw No. 14797 be now abandoned.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby council refers CFIB letter on PST expansion to staff for review
minutes_briefs.summary: Council voted unanimously to refer a letter from the Canadian Federation of Independent Business (CFIB) to city staff for further review. The letter urged Council to oppose the Provincial Sales Tax (PST) expansion on professional services. Staff will now study the issue and report back to Council with their findings. No decision has been made yet on whether Burnaby will take a formal position against the PST expansion.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required. Monitor for the staff report and any future council decision on this matter.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
12.1 Item 3.1 Council Correspondence and Public Submission Package Dated April 14, 2026
Councillor Lee referred to Item 3.1 of the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submission Package from the Canadian Federation of Independent Business urging Council to oppose the Provincial Sales Tax expansion on professional services.

Arising from discussion, the following motion was brought forward:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2025 - 186
THAT item 3.1 of the Council Correspondence and Public Submission Package dated April 14, 2026, from the Canadian Federation of Independent Business urging Council to oppose the Provincial Sales Tax Expansion on professional service, be REFERRED to staff for further review and report back to Council.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby Council approved 2025 Annual Municipal Report and opened it for public comment
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the 2025 Annual Municipal Report, which includes the city's audited financial statements for the year ending December 31, 2025. The report and a highlights document are now available for public review and comment starting April 15, 2026. This is a routine annual reporting requirement that gives residents and business owners a chance to see how the city spent its money and to provide feedback.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and Highlights on the city's website and submit any comments before the public comment period closes.

key_dates.label: Public comment period opens for 2025 Annual Municipal Report

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Resolution No. C2026 - 036
THAT the report titled “2025 Annual Municipal Report and 2025 Annual Municipal Report Highlights”, dated for April 7, 2026, of the Audit Committee meeting, be received for information;
THAT the 2025 Annual Municipal Report (Attachment 1), which includes the audited Consolidated Financial Statements, for the year ended December 31, 2025, be approved; and
THAT the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and 2025 Annual Municipal Report Highlights documents be made available to the public for commentary starting on April 15, 2026.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
